steps.title: Build two table points
steps.content: Use Desmos regression instead of doing the whole model by hand. The problem gives the first point directly: when $x=0$, $y=90$. A $20\%$ increase means the next value is $120\%$ of the old value, so when $x=1$, $y=1.20(90)=108$.

steps.title: Run exponential regression
steps.content: Type $y_1\sim a b^{x_1}$. Desmos returns $a=90$ and $b=1.20$, which means the model is $y=90(1.20)^x$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Choice A has the starting value $90$ and the growth factor $1.20$. The other choices either use the wrong starting value or the wrong multiplier, so choice A is correct.
